runs.ce_name: Warner Bros. Studio Tour London Tickets
runs.root_cause: CVR improved +0.31pp overall (C2O +4.50pp from Easter/spring high-intent planners + price dip on main product), but S2C fell −1.13pp concentrated on mobile — near-term weekend slots selling out 2–3 days in advance left last-minute mobile visitors with no available dates. Structural S2C gap vs LY (~15–17pp below) suggests tighter WBSL allotment management in 2026 vs 2025.

Vatican Museums CVR fell −0.63pp (4.47%→3.84%) because peak spring demand (+13% traffic) exceeded fixed timed-entry slot supply — shrinking available booking dates by 5.1 per 30-day window — while a secondary checkout failure on iOS (C2A) and Android (A2O) added a compounding −40% Shapley contribution. Second run under v1.6 rubric: identified fixed segment inconsistency (cascade declared Google Ads but queries ran at Paid level) and missing user counts in lead-time and device C2O tables.

S2C fell from 31.8% to 25.2% (−6.65pp, 70% of ΔCVR) because the post period captured the wind-down of Hawaii's spring break season — US domestic (83% of traffic, S2C −6.8pp) and Canadian visitors were replaced by lower-intent off-peak tourists, while international non-spring-break visitors held flat. No supply, pricing, or UX change confirmed — purely demand quality decline.
